Package org.apache.juneau.marshall.sse

Class SseEvent

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.marshall.sse.SseEvent
@BeanType(properties="event,data,id,retry") public class SseEvent extends Object
Represents a single Server-Sent Event (SSE) frame on the wire.

Each event maps to a sequence of field: value lines terminated by a blank line in the text/event-stream wire format:

event: progress data: {"step":4,"total":10} id: 42 retry: 5000

The payload field data is a pre-serialized String — the SSE serializer does not compose with a delegate marshaller. Callers that need a typed payload should serialize it themselves (e.g. via Json.of(x)) and assign the resulting string to setData(...). The serializer will split that string on \n into multiple data: lines per spec.

Example: build and serialize an event:

SseEvent event = new SseEvent() .setEvent("progress") .setData(Json.of(Map.of("step", 4, "total", 10))) .setId("42") .setRetry(5000L); String wire = Sse.of(event);

Notes:
  • This class is not thread safe.
See Also:
  • SSE Basics
  • WHATWG HTML §9.2 — Server-sent events

      public static final String DEFAULT_EVENT

  • Constructor Details

    • SseEvent

      public SseEvent()
      Constructor.

    • SseEvent

      public SseEvent(String event, String data)
      Constructor.
      Parameters:
      event - The event-type name. Can be null (dispatches as "message").
      data - The payload string. Can be null (no data: lines emitted).
